output.title: Security Deposit Recovery: How to Write a Demand Email When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
output.meta_description: Frustrated your landlord is withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and how to send a demand letter to recover your deposit fast.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (And Why They Might Be Wrong)
Tenant Rights: What a Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct
Drafting Your Security Deposit Dispute Demand Email: Subject Lines That Create Urgency
Body Copy That Gets Results: State Your Case and Set a Deadline
What to Do If Your Landlord Still Ignores Your Demand
key_points: Subject lines should be direct and legal in tone, e.g., 'Formal Demand: Immediate Return of Security Deposit — [Your Name/Unit]'.
Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14–30 days; missing these deadlines may entitle you to penalties or treble damages.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear—only documented damages beyond ordinary use and unpaid rent.
A written demand email serves as powerful legal evidence if you need to escalate to small claims court.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes and send it today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return a security deposit and need a fast, legally sound way to recover their money.
